rightPanel.reviewSessionDesc: Review the latest file changes captured in the current session. Expand a file to inspect the diff.
rightPanel.reviewEmptyDesc: Latest diffs are unavailable. File changes for this session will appear here.
rightPanel.browserEmptyState: Enter a URL above to browse
rightPanel.orchestrationTasksEmpty: This run has no standalone task list
rightPanel.orchestrationToolCallsEmpty: This member has no tool calls to display
rightPanel.acpDesc: Best for architecture-controlled work before implementation. The main agent clarifies requirements, defines boundaries, designs the approach, decomposes tasks, and synthesizes results; concrete implementation is executed by sub-agents.
rightPanel.acpRuleNoCode: The main agent does not write implementation code or modify files directly. It first clarifies goals, constraints, risks, and acceptance criteria.
rightPanel.acpRuleDelegate: Implementation tasks must be delegated through Task / SubAgent / teammate to the appropriate sub-agent.
rightPanel.acpRuleReport: Each sub-task should return a structured Markdown summary so the main agent can review, synthesize, and continue execution.
rightPanel.acpNextActionClarify: Fill in goals, constraints, boundaries, and acceptance criteria before planning.
rightPanel.acpNextActionPlan: Continue refining the implementation plan, save it, then exit Plan Mode.
rightPanel.acpNextActionExecute: Keep decomposing the approved plan into tasks, delegate sub-agents, and synthesize their outputs.

clarify.summary: Use this when the request is still fuzzy and you want the agent to slow down, ask better follow-up questions, and align on the target before acting.
solves: Unclear requirements, incomplete context, or multiple possible interpretations
Need help turning rough ideas into concrete tasks, specs, or acceptance criteria
Want the agent to challenge assumptions before it starts making changes

cowork.summary: The agent works as a collaborative partner: it explains its approach, follows a Plan-Act-Observe loop, and handles both coding and non-coding tasks like research, DevOps, or project setup. The right panel opens automatically for step tracking.
solves: Multi-step workflows mixing research, planning, file changes, and terminal commands — the agent explains each step
Tasks beyond pure coding: project setup, documentation, DevOps, analysis, or anything requiring coordination
Situations where you want visibility into the agent's thought process and intermediate results

code.summary: The agent acts as a focused pair programmer. It reads before editing, matches your codebase style, makes surgical changes, and keeps explanations minimal — letting the code speak for itself.
solves: Precise implementation: writing features, fixing bugs, refactoring, or completing modules with engineering discipline
When you want clean code output with minimal narration — terse confirmation instead of verbose walk-throughs
Changes that demand strict attention to existing conventions, types, imports, and API contracts

acp.summary: Architecture Control Planning is for complex work that needs control before implementation: clarify goals and boundaries first, then design the architecture, decompose execution, and delegate delivery. The main agent does not code directly.
solves: Complex tasks that require background, scope, constraints, dependencies, and acceptance criteria to be clarified before implementation
Work where the main agent should focus on architecture, orchestration, and process control instead of coding directly
Cases where sub-tasks should run in parallel and return structured Markdown summaries for continued coordination

resourcesPage.title: Resources
resourcesPage.subtitle: Manage SubAgents and Commands in one place
resourcesPage.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, summary, or path
resourcesPage.emptyDesc: Try switching type or adjusting the search query
resourcesPage.readonlyNotice: This is a bundled resource. Preview is available, but direct editing is disabled.
resourcesPage.addCommandDesc: Enter a kebab-case command name. It will be created in the user commands directory and opened in edit mode.
resourcesPage.commandNameHint: Only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens are allowed, for example release-note.
resourcesPage.selectDesc: Use the left side to switch resource type, then choose an item from the list.

drawPage.title: AI Drawing
drawPage.subtitle: Aggregates image models across providers and supports official Gemini image generation
drawPage.modelSectionDesc: Choose an image model from your configured providers
drawPage.promptSectionDesc: Describe the image you want to generate
drawPage.promptPlaceholder: For example: an orange cat wearing a space helmet on a neon rainy Tokyo street, cinematic lighting, ultra detailed
drawPage.promptHint: Describing subject, scene, style, lighting, camera, and details usually improves image quality and consistency.
drawPage.pasteImageHint: Paste or drop an image for editing, or use the image button above.
drawPage.promptCoreSuggestionPlaceholder: e.g. emphasize loneliness, keep realistic photography, do not change the outfit
drawPage.promptCoreSuggestionHint: Prompt optimization prioritizes this suggestion and folds it into the optimized result.
drawPage.promptStyleHint: Combine multiple styles; the optimizer blends them into one coherent visual language instead of stacking tags.
drawPage.optimizeDialogHint: Start optimization directly, or add a core suggestion first to steer the rewrite.
drawPage.imageQualityHint: Applies to OpenAI Images / Responses image generation.
drawPage.imageQualityUnsupported: Current model does not support quality
drawPage.streamUnsupported: Current model does not support real-time preview
drawPage.resultSectionDesc: Recent image generations and errors appear here
drawPage.currentResultSectionDesc: Shows only the latest result by default; active generations take priority.
drawPage.historySectionDesc: Earlier drawing records are collected here
drawPage.historyEmptyDesc: After a second generation, older results move into history.
drawPage.emptyDesc: Select an image model and enter a prompt. Generated images will appear here.
drawPage.noModelsDesc: Enable at least one image model in provider settings, especially models with category image.
drawPage.providerDisabled: The selected provider is disabled. Enable it in settings before generating.
drawPage.modelDisabled: The selected model is disabled. Enable it in settings before generating.
drawPage.authRequired: Authentication is required for the selected image provider. Configure credentials first.
drawPage.noModel: No usable image model configuration was found.
drawPage.unknownError: Generation failed. Please try again later.
drawPage.noImageOutput: The model returned no image output.
drawPage.optimizePromptDesc: Refine subject, composition, lighting, and fidelity constraints from a GPT Image prompt-engineering perspective while preserving your original intent.
drawPage.optimizeUnavailable: No fast text model is currently available.
drawPage.gifPromptSectionDesc: Fill the fixed fields, generate a 3x3 grid image, then slice and convert it into a GIF automatically.
drawPage.gifCharacterPlaceholder: e.g. silver-haired mech girl, full body, blue glowing armor
drawPage.gifReferenceHint: Upload exactly one reference image. The system keeps the character appearance consistent; extra character notes are optional.
drawPage.gifCharacterOptionalPlaceholder: e.g. keep cat ears, cape, glowing texture
drawPage.gifStylePlaceholder: e.g. cyberpunk, soft gradients, clean background
drawPage.gifActionPlaceholder: e.g. standing, then waving, then blinking, with smooth continuous motion
drawPage.gifRulesHint: The system enforces one character, exact 3x3 slicing, a consistent camera/background, and a single simple motion made of small continuous changes between adjacent frames.
drawPage.gifTransparentProviderHint: If the current model type is OpenAI Images, the system automatically requests a transparent PNG background.
drawPage.gifOptimizeDisabledHint: Prompt optimization is disabled in GIF mode to preserve the fixed template constraints.
drawPage.gifCharacterRequired: Character description is required for text-to-GIF mode.
drawPage.gifStyleRequired: Style description is required in GIF mode.
drawPage.gifActionRequired: Action description is required in GIF mode.
drawPage.gifReferenceRequired: Reference mode requires exactly one reference image.
